index.description: L2 index of methodologies in the ba domain. The retriever reads this after picking ba from L1 domains.xml. Entries list slug + tier + summary; open the leaf AGENTS.md / content/*.xml for full payload.

methodology.summary: Explicit, testable Given-When-Then or rule-based criteria per user story with mapping to ≥1 test case and explicit edge / negative / non-functional coverage.
Produces stable, testable acceptance criteria (Given/When/Then or rule-based) with cross-reference IDs (AC-FEATURE-NN) tying spec, test-plan, and CI report.
Produces a 45-minute discovery-call playbook output: budget range, decision-maker, fit score, and next-step recommendation with verbatim quotes.
Map BA competencies onto Scrum ceremonies + SAFe levels (Team / Program / Solution / Portfolio) — produces a `spec` of which BA activity fires when.

Picks the right Agile-BA framework (Scrum-BA, SAFe-BA, Disciplined Agile, Dual-Track) for a given team shape + cadence + regulatory bar, with named per-sprint BA activities and IIBA Agile-Extension 7-principle fit-report.

Produces a per-story checklist that vets AI-generated acceptance criteria for plausible-but-wrong shapes: invented happy paths, missing edge cases, fabricated metrics, contradictory constraints, before ACs enter sprint.
Reviewer rubric that scores LLM-drafted acceptance criteria across happy/negative/edge/perf/a11y dimensions and gates them on evidence + frozen weights.
BABOK-grounded methodology for BAs to draft user stories + AC from elicitation artifacts via LLM with hallucination guards (compliance-domain rules, evidence per claim).
Reusable prompt-skeleton + slot library for BA elicitation tasks (interview prep, follow-up probes, paraphrase-back validation) with structural validation per call.

Produces a methodology spec for the business-analyst role on AI-assisted workflows: governance, agentic-system specs, AI TRiSM compliance, and the BA-specific checklists feeding requirements / AC / story workflows.

Produces a methodology spec for AI-assisted BA workflows: requirements extraction via NLP, stakeholder sentiment analysis, process discovery, AI-accelerated BA docs, agentic system specs, with AI TRiSM compliance gates.

Produces a per-story checklist that vets AI-generated user stories for plausible-but-wrong shapes: invented personas, conflated jobs, fabricated metrics, missing edge cases — before they enter the backlog.

Produces a traceable requirement record from a stakeholder-interview transcript: requirement statement, source quote, stakeholder owner, ambiguity flag, and link to the verbatim moment in the transcript.

Decomposes vague AI-product asks ('add chatbot', 'make it smart') into INVEST-compliant user stories with explicit AI-vs-deterministic boundary, eval criteria (precision/recall/cost/latency), fallback behavior, and golden-set seed.

Scans a requirements doc against a closed checklist (vague quantifiers / undefined terms / contradictory ACs / missing edge-cases / unowned decisions) and emits a per-finding report with severity, evidence quote, and remediation owner.
Establishes decision rights (RACI), change-control workflow, and stakeholder communication plan for a requirements stream before requirements work starts.
Five-day, artefact-driven onboarding plan that gets a BA from "first commit" to "ready to write requirements" — day-level outputs committed to a week-one-pack/ folder.
Pre-elicitation 6-step framework: select plan-driven/change-driven/hybrid, name stakeholders, schedule elicitation, define deliverables, set governance approver + change process + escalation path.
Produces five BABOK KA1 task artefacts (T1-T5) with named approvers, cadences, and lifecycles seeding all downstream BA work.
Produces a requirements-management register (change-control, traceability, approval thresholds) governing how requirements evolve through the SDLC.
3-bullet BA-specific standup script (clarifications-needed / AC-ready / blockers) replacing dev-style updates, captured as a typed daily artefact.
Evolution checklist that lifts a BA from documentation-only execution to strategic-partner stance — quarterly OKR co-ownership, business-case authorship, named sponsor mapping.
Produces a stance review naming what BA-as-partner means in this engagement — decision rights, escalation paths, value contracts.
Sprint-end BA → QA handoff packet (story, AC count, fixture refs, edge cases, named QA owner) replacing ad-hoc verbal/Slack handoffs.

Read-only routing index of 2025-2026 BA trends (AI-enabled decisions, outcome-driven strategy, responsible innovation, operational resilience, digital trust, sustainability) mapped to internal methodology.
Produces a quarterly checklist mapping current BA-discipline trends (AI-enabled, product-ops, value-stream) to concrete adoption decisions for the engagement.
Post-launch 90-day checklist verifying realized benefits match projected business case (metric, target, measured-value, variance, named owner).
Pre-bid 0-100 weighted rubric across 6 dimensions (fit / margin / risk / timeline / team / strategic) with go/no-go threshold and named approver.
Two-pass BPMN authoring: LLM drafts BPMN 2.0 XML from process transcript, named SME reviews + signs + commits — never AI-only commit.
Five-stage BPA loop (identify → document → analyze → design → validate) under portfolio-agent orchestration that produces auditor-grade as-is/to-be process models with NVA-percentage baselines.
5-stage analysis cycle that turns raw process evidence into a current-state map, a value/time/cost analysis, and a future-state diff ready for automation or redesign.
Scores a Change Request by blast radius (requirements / AC / tests / regulatory clauses / dependent stories touched) and routes it to the correct approver (BA / PO / steering committee).
Maps client-supplied compliance control IDs (HIPAA, PCI, SOX) to internal requirements + tests so an auditor can trace any control to passing evidence on demand.
Domain-indexed compliance checklist (GDPR / HIPAA / PSD2 / SOC2 / WCAG) BAs apply during requirements validation to catch missing non-functional and regulatory items before sign-off.
Produces a compliance traceability pack for a regulated BA engagement (KYC / FHIR / GDPR / SOC2): requirement-to-regulation map, evidence inventory, control owner table, audit-ready exports.
A self-contained contractor brief — context, deliverable spec, acceptance criteria, evidence checkpoints, rate + payment terms — that a subcontractor can execute without back-and-forth.
Standardised Change Request impact memo: scope delta, cost delta, schedule delta, risk delta, recommendation — produced by BA, signed by approver, attached to the CR record.
Major-CR options matrix: ≥2 viable responses (accept / reduce / defer / re-baseline) with cost / value / risk per option, recommendation, and approver routing.

BA-grade data analysis pipeline (purpose → elements → model → quality → rules) producing a data dictionary, six-dimension quality assessment, and validation/derivation/default/constraint/uniqueness rule set linked to requirements.
Identify, define, and document data needs before system development — data dictionary, conceptual/logical model, quality dimensions, and business rules as a versioned contract.

Evidence-based requirements pipeline: every requirement anchored to a business question, quantified baseline metric, measurable success target, and instrumentation plan that closes the loop after launch.
Produces a requirements set derived from observed data signals (analytics, telemetry, support tickets) with explicit evidence trails per requirement.

Six-step structured evaluation (define → generate → weight → score → sensitivity → document) yielding an auditable decision record with traceability to elicited requirements and reconciled stakeholder weights.
6-step decision-matrix process — define decision, options, weighted criteria locked pre-scoring, evidence-per-cell scores, sensitivity Monte Carlo, signed rationale.
Standardised options memo BAs use to escalate decisions: option framing, cost-value-risk per option, recommendation, dissent acknowledgement, decision-required-by date.
Captures decision rationale in a versioned record: decision, alternatives considered, evidence, dissent, named owner — so the program can answer 'why did we do X?' a year later.
A library of canonical Definitions of Done by deliverable type (story / feature / release / contractor task) so BAs and POs can reuse battle-tested AC patterns instead of reinventing.
Outsource-grade DoR checklist gating stories before grooming: covers engineering readiness plus client-side dependencies (env access, data, regulator/legal/security sign-off).
Sprint-demo recap email skeleton that pins decisions made, scope deltas, open items, and named follow-ups so the client cannot retroactively reinterpret the demo.
Formal handover protocol from pre-sales discovery BA to delivery BA: artefact bundle, walkthrough script, signed acknowledgement, and post-handover hot-line.
Reusable BA pack for any e-commerce checkout: user-flow, edge-case catalogue, NFR thresholds, regulatory checklist, payment-provider integration spec.

Triangulated elicitation pipeline (interview/workshop/observation/survey/prototype/focus-group/brainstorm) producing typed session artifacts and REQ stubs that cite ≥2 distinct techniques per requirement.
Produces a technique-to-session mapping selecting interview / workshop / survey / observation / document-analysis per information type and stakeholder count.
Rubric of six facilitation anti-patterns BA workshops must catch in real time, with detection cues + named counter-moves that defuse without humiliation.

Produces a domain-specific BA pack for a FinTech KYC engagement: regulation-anchored requirements (FATF / AML / CDD), compliance traceability, identity-evidence inventory, and PEP / sanctions-screen control map.
Produces a fixed-price risk loading model: risk-register-to-buffer translation, contingency tiers, hidden-cost classes, and a defensible bid number with explicit risk-pricing reasoning per category.

Produces a change-request playbook for P4 outsource BAs at the BA/procurement boundary: classify each CR by contract type (fixed-price vs T&M), apply absorb / negotiate-addendum / scope-swap decisions, and price residual risk explicitly.
Solo / freelance proposal skeleton: scope envelope, assumptions, exclusions, payment milestones, change-request rate, anti-scope-creep language.
Generates a 30-minute freelancer discovery call template with a pre-filled question bank, a go/no-go scorecard, and a one-page proposal seed.

Operator-grounded as-is validation: ≥2 frontline operators per variant via shadowing/gemba/listening-session, closed observation taxonomy, reconcile-not-replace synthesis blocking sponsor-pleasing model edits.
Domain glossary kept as versioned YAML next to code: terms, definitions, synonyms, deprecated aliases, owner, last-reviewed date.

Living-doc glossary lifecycle (intake → definition → conflict resolution → deprecation → propagation) producing a single-source-of-truth term registry with audit-able conflict-resolution records and term-usage hooks across docs/code/tests.

Produces a public-sector BA pack — FOIA/records-retention schedule, WCAG AA accessibility audit plan, procurement-clause review log, and comment-period buffer — that closes the design-time gaps that bite GovTech launches.

Produces a HealthTech BA pack — FHIR resources (Patient, Encounter, Observation, Condition), HIPAA/GDPR overlap matrix, IRB/consent workflow, and audit-trail spec — so clinical features ship without a post-launch privacy incident.
Pre-engagement HIPAA Business Associate Agreement (BAA) checklist for every PHI-touching vendor: data flow, breach notification, sub-processor map, audit rights.
Five-question qualification rubric for inbound consulting leads: budget, decider, timeline, scope clarity, fit; emits qualify / reject / discovery-call routing.
Per-interface specification pipeline (catalog → classify → specify → reconcile) producing the integration landscape: data elements, protocol, frequency, volume, security, error-handling per boundary.
Inventory + contract analysis of every connection point (API, file, message bus, UI, hardware) with named owner, data shape, SLA, and error contract per interface.
Pipeline configuration for BA interview recordings: transcript generation, automated PII redaction, manual review checkpoint, retention policy, access log.
Reference catalog for all six BABOK Knowledge Areas (KA-1..KA-6) producing a per-KA task table, canonical workflow sequence, and routing map back into the BA methodology library.
One-page L1 routing index over BABOK's six Knowledge Areas (KA-1..KA-6) producing a triage decision: which KA owns the current ask + which methodology to load next.
Lightweight KPI drift alarm: baseline window, deviation threshold, alert routing, suppression window, escalation path; runs against any time-series KPI.
Pattern: BA drafts tickets live on a shared screen during elicitation, contributor signs off in-session, eliminating async clarification rounds.
Catalog of 12 enterprise BA frameworks across KA-1..KA-6 with per-framework usage notes, deliverable templates, and trigger conditions for choosing each over its peers.
Perspective routing wrapper over BABOK that maps the ask to a perspective (agile, AI/ML, cloud-native, platform, BI, BizArch, BPM) and adapts KA tasks + deliverables accordingly.
Produces a framework-selection decision (BABOK / Lean BA / Product-Ops BA / Hybrid) with explicit fit criteria for the engagement context.
Extracts obligations from NDAs and MSAs into a structured register: obligation, owner, trigger, deadline, evidence pointer, audit-ready.

Event-log driven process discovery + automation candidate scoring (volume × frequency × variance × rule-density) producing a ranked automation pipeline with RPA/IDP/orchestration verdict per candidate.
Produces an automation-assessment report ranking discovered process variants by frequency × variance × cost and naming candidate automations with feasibility scores.
Decision-grade shortlist of process-mining tools (Celonis / UiPath PM / Disco / open-source) with weighted criteria, evidence anchors, and named owner.
Run-book for remote/hybrid BA workshops (pre-read floor, four-role breakouts, camera policy, time-zone split, async pulses) producing the workshop run-book + canvas templates + ground rules block.
Deterministic scorecard for every requirement before baseline: atomicity, testability, traceability, value, ambiguity, completeness — 0/1/2 per axis with rules.
Hierarchical requirements documentation pipeline (BR → SH → SR with FR/NFR split) producing typed BR/SH/SR records with acceptance criteria, source citations, and version-controlled artifacts.
Produces SRS / BRD / user-story documents conforming to a checked schema, with traceability IDs, acceptance criteria, and review state per requirement.

Per-requirement state machine (Draft → Approved → Implemented → Verified) with change-control gates, multi-role sign-off, and immutable version log producing audit-grade traceability of every status transition.
Produces a lifecycle policy declaring states (draft / reviewed / approved / implemented / verified / retired), transitions, owners, and SLAs per requirement.
Method-locked prioritization pipeline (MoSCoW / RICE / Kano / WSJF) with stakeholder weights, cost-coupled scoring, and decision-record output blocking method-shopping and HiPPO bias.
Produces a ranked requirements table using MoSCoW / RICE / WSJF with explicit per-requirement scores, weights, and rationale.

Bidirectional RTM pipeline (forward coverage need→test, backward justification test→need) producing per-requirement trace records over consistent role vocabulary suitable for automated coverage analysis.
Produces a Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) linking each requirement forward to design / code / test / release artefacts and backward to source elicitation.

Four-stage validation pipeline (correctness check → completeness check → technique match → segmented review) producing a sign-off pack with quality scores, dissent log, and explicit gate before baseline.
Produces a validation report confirming each requirement meets value-alignment, feasibility, and review criteria before sign-off.
Indexed pack of polite-firm push-back phrasings for 7 recurring scope-creep moments; the founder pastes from it instead of wording each reply.
Per-engagement intake-and-classify gate that converts every new ask into change-order, no-op, in-scope-clarified, or backlog — never silently absorbed.
Live-meeting protocol absorbing client asks (verbatim capture, visible canvas, no-live-judgement) with 24h triage SLA + 48h response SLA producing a triaged parking-lot artefact and response log.
Five quantitative drift metrics (CR velocity, AC rewrite, story split, churn ratio, parking-lot age) with thresholds + named trigger actions catching scope drift weeks before re-baseline.
Structured 4-type solution evaluation (design / implementation / deployment / post-implementation) with baseline-locked metrics, 30/60/90-day cadence, and accept-with-remediation-or-reject verdict.
Produces a post-implementation review comparing delivered solution against baselined requirements, value drivers, and KPIs with explicit gap callouts.
Fixed-price SoW where every scope clause, exclusion, and pricing assumption carries an [A-NN] ID pointing at the discovery artefact that set it.
Identifies and classifies stakeholders by power × interest, defines engagement strategy per quadrant, and produces a named RACI + communications plan that owners actually follow.
Produces a stakeholder register + power/interest map + RACI matrix mapping who decides, contributes, signs off, and must be informed across the engagement.
60-minute structured five-phase meeting (frame → positions → rubric → decide → record dissent) that converts a requirement disagreement into a signed decision-log entry.
In-the-moment five-step loop (pause → reframe → isolate → propose → confirm) for the BA when a stakeholder conflict erupts mid-requirements-review.
Produces a business-need statement + gap analysis + change strategy tying current state to future state with measurable closure plan.
Identifies and quantifies the business need behind an initiative — problem statement, evidence, success metric, and the question 'what if we do nothing?' — before any solutioning starts.
Bridges current and future state with a sequenced change strategy: solution options, transition states, organisational readiness checks, and a recommended path with cost-risk-time trade-offs.
Documents the as-is environment — processes, systems, capabilities, pain points, constraints — with named owners and evidence so future-state and gap analysis have a real baseline.
Defines the target to-be environment — processes, systems, capabilities, measurable outcomes, and design constraints — anchored to the business need and reachable from current state.
Identifies the delta between current and future state — capability gaps, process gaps, system gaps — with severity and remediation owner, feeding the change strategy.
Produces a glossary + decision-tree primer that grounds BA team vocabulary (mission, vision, OKR, SMART, strategy vs tactics) before any strategic artefact is authored.
Produces a method-selection record (Porter Five Forces / SWOT / Wardley / OST / Blue Ocean) with explicit fit criteria, scoring, and limitation register.
Automated traceability-graph maintenance: a daily job that scans tickets, commits, and tests; rebuilds the requirement-to-deliverable graph; emits broken-link alerts to a named owner.
Ready-to-use CSV traceability matrix + Jira export script for weekly refresh; closes the gap between BA discovery and engineering execution.
Comparison + setup-guide report for traceability tooling (Jira, Azure DevOps, Polarion, Confluence): link types, custom fields, trace gates, gotchas.
Defines actor-goal-scenario use cases — main success path, alternate flows, exception flows, preconditions, postconditions — that downstream teams turn into stories, tests, and screens.
Express functional requirements as named-actor → system interaction sequences (main scenario + alternative + exception flows) with preconditions, postconditions, and trigger per use case.
Two-axis story map (user journey × release slices) producing a backlog with explicit walking-skeleton, MVP, and release-N horizons so teams ship value rather than features.
Arrange stories along horizontal user-journey activities + vertical priority bands (R1 walking skeleton through R4 nice-to-have) as YAML-in-git for release planning.
